questions.prompt: How many scored items does the PSI Oregon CCB outline assign to the Bidding and Estimating domain?
questions.explanation: The PSI Candidate Information Bulletin lists Bidding and Estimating as a separate domain with 6 scored items, so it is a dedicated part of exam preparation.

questions.prompt: A residential job starts at $1,900 and later increases to $2,600. Under ORS 701.305, when must the written contract be delivered or mailed?
choices: Within five days after the contractor knew or should have known the threshold was exceeded
Only if the owner requests it in writing
questions.explanation: ORS 701.305 requires a written contract once aggregate residential price exceeds $2,000, including a five-day catch-up delivery-or-mailing rule when the threshold is crossed during performance.

questions.prompt: Which statement best matches CCB guidance on pricing models?
choices: Time-and-materials guarantees a final total at signing
Fixed-price contracts always forbid change orders
Fixed-price contracts state a defined overall price, while time-and-materials may leave the final total unknown at signing
Oregon law bans time-and-materials residential contracts
questions.explanation: CCB consumer guidance distinguishes fixed-price and time-and-materials by price certainty at contract formation; fixed-price states a defined total while time-and-materials may not.
